comment: Synthetic demo personas. Names/phones live ONLY in this harness file — the Cupid backend stays anonymous-by-design (hashed phones, no names). Pairs are engineered against computeCompatibility(): Maya+Eli ~93, Priya+Marcus ~73, Jordan+Sam ~56 (borderline), Tessa+Rob blocked (smoking dealbreaker), Diane+Walt blocked (age range), Chloe+Andre ~15 (intent mismatch).

personas.name: Maya
personas.blurb: 29, pediatric nurse. Half of the high-score pair with Eli (~93).

conversation.content: My sister said I should try this. I'm Maya
Hey Maya! Smart sister. I'm Cupid — I get to know you through conversation, then introduce you to people you'd actually click with. So what does a good weekend look like for you?
Farmers market in the morning, then probably trying a new recipe or hitting a trail at Castlewood if the weather's good
Cooking AND hiking — noted. Are you usually dragging friends along, or is solo time part of the appeal?
Honestly I recharge alone. I love people but in small doses ha
An introvert who can hold her own at a farmers market. Got it. Last thing — any hard nos I should know about?

personas.name: Eli
personas.blurb: 31, architect. Half of the high-score pair with Maya (~93).

conversation.content: Heard about this from a coworker. Worth a shot
Welcome! Low effort, high signal — that's the pitch. I'm Cupid. Tell me something you did recently that you'd want to do again.
Caught a show at Off Broadway last week, and I've been perfecting a cacio e pepe. Also always down for a hike
Live music, cooking, trails — you're making my job easy. Are you looking for something serious, or seeing where things go?
Serious. I'm 31, done with the situationship phase
Respect. Long-term it is. I've got a strong picture of you already — I'll be in touch when I find someone worth your time.

personas.name: Priya
personas.blurb: 33, product manager. High pair with Marcus (~73).

conversation.content: OK I'm intrigued. How does this work?
Simple: we talk, I learn what makes you tick, and I only introduce you to people I genuinely think fit. No swiping. What fills your non-work hours?
Training for the GO! half marathon, planning a Portugal trip, and dragging friends to wineries in Augusta
A runner with a passport and good taste. What matters most to you in a person — not hobbies, character?
Kindness first. And someone who's still growing — I can't do coasting
Kindness and momentum. Noted and respected. I'll start looking.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.blurb: 35, high school teacher. High pair with Priya (~73).

conversation.content: A friend swears by this so here I am
Good friends give good referrals. I'm Cupid — think of me as the friend who actually listens before setting you up. What's your idea of a great first date?
Honestly? A long walk, maybe ending at a jazz spot. I'd rather talk than perform
Substance over spectacle. What do you do, and does it feed you or just pay you?
I teach high school history. Exhausting but it feeds me. I also run most mornings
A teacher who runs at dawn and loves jazz. You're going to be easy to match well. Give me a little time.

personas.name: Jordan
personas.blurb: 27, UX designer. Borderline pair with Sam (~56).

conversation.content: yo. my roommate set this up for me lol
Roommates with initiative — love it. I'm Cupid. Paint me a picture: it's Friday night, what are you doing?
board game night with the crew or bingeing anime. sometimes a long bike ride saturday morning to make up for it
Catan or something heavier? And are you looking for a relationship, or open to seeing what happens?
wingspan guy actually. and yeah open — not in a rush but not against something real
Open it is. Wingspan respect. I'll keep an eye out for someone who'd hold their own at your table.

personas.name: Sam
personas.blurb: 26, freelance illustrator. Borderline pair with Jordan (~56). Zero credits — paywall demo when her proposal lands.

conversation.content: Trying this because apps are a nightmare
You're among friends — that nightmare is exactly why I exist. I'm Cupid. What does your week actually look like?
I freelance illustration so my schedule's mine. Yoga most mornings, paint commissions till late, board games when friends pry me out
And to be clear I want something real, not a texting pen pal
An independent creative who knows what she wants — noted, long-term. Anything that's an instant no for you?
Flakiness. And anyone who calls my job a hobby
Reliability and respect. The bar should be that high. I'm on it.

personas.name: Tessa
personas.blurb: 30, physical therapist. Would score ~95 with Rob — blocked by her smoking dealbreaker. The money demo for hard filters.

conversation.content: Hi! Friend recommended you
Welcome! I'm Cupid — matchmaking by conversation, not swiping. What would your friends say you're about?
They'd say I'm outside every chance I get. PT by day, trails and concerts otherwise. I cook a lot too
Active and feeds people — strong combination. Any absolute dealbreakers I should respect?
Smoking. Hard no, not negotiable. I work in rehab, I've seen too much
Smoking is off the table, period. Everything else sounds refreshingly open. I'll find you someone who earns it.

personas.name: Rob
personas.blurb: 32, brewery operations manager. Tessa's near-perfect match on paper — but he smokes. Demonstrates the dealbreaker filter.

conversation.content: Alright let's see what the robot matchmaker can do
Challenge accepted. I'm Cupid. Start me off — what's a perfect Saturday?
Early hike, cook something big for whoever shows up, catch a show at the Pageant if anyone's playing. Cigarette on the porch in between, my one vice
A host, a hiker, and honest about the vice — all useful to know. Looking for something lasting?
Yeah, the real thing. I'm done with halfway
The real thing it is. I'll be straight with you like you were with me — I only make intros I believe in.

personas.name: Diane
personas.blurb: 45, restaurant owner. Demonstrates the age-range hard filter — Walt's preference tops out at 44.

conversation.content: My daughter signed me up. Be gentle
Your daughter has excellent judgment. I'm Cupid, and this is just a conversation — no profiles, no photos to agonize over. What brings you joy these days?
My restaurant, my garden, and a good glass of wine at the Muny in summer
A woman who builds things — a business, a garden, an evening. What are you hoping to find?
Someone settled. 40s to mid 50s, knows who he is, makes me laugh
Settled, self-aware, funny. A high bar that the right person will clear easily. Leave it with me.

personas.name: Walt
personas.blurb: 52, retired firefighter. Seeks 38-44 — Diane is 45, one year outside. Shows bidirectional age filtering.

conversation.content: Retired last year. Figured it's time to stop being a hermit
Thirty years of service and now some time for yourself — welcome. I'm Cupid. What's filling the days now?
Golf league, season tickets at the Fox, learning more about wine than a firefighter probably should
The Fox and a wine habit — retirement suits you. What kind of person are you hoping to meet?
Someone late 30s to mid 40s, loyal, likes a night out. I'm a one-woman man
Loyal and lively, late 30s to mid 40s. Clear and reasonable. I'll get to work.

personas.name: Chloe
personas.blurb: 28, event coordinator. Casual intent vs Andre's long-term — shows intent scoring. Zero credits for the paywall demo.

conversation.content: hiii ok what is this exactly
Hi! I'm Cupid — an AI matchmaker you just... talk to. No profiles. So talk to me: what's your scene?
festivals, dancing, anywhere with live music and good light for photos. I plan events for work so my weekends are usually GO time
High energy, camera in hand. Are you looking for something serious right now, or keeping it light?
light!! I just got out of something long. fun first, we'll see after
Fun first, no pressure — totally valid chapter. I'll look for someone who can keep up.

personas.name: Andre
personas.blurb: 30, librarian. Long-term intent, quiet interests — scores ~15 with Chloe. Shows why intent alignment is weighted 30%.

conversation.content: Hello. A patron at the library recommended this, oddly enough
Library word-of-mouth might be my favorite referral channel yet. I'm Cupid. What does a good evening look like for you?
A novel, a phone call with someone interesting, or the chess club on Thursdays. The art museum on weekends
A reader who'd rather call than text — increasingly rare. What are you ultimately looking for?
Marriage, eventually. Family. I see no reason to pretend otherwise
Clarity like that saves everyone time. Long-term, family-minded. I'll choose carefully.
